verdict.answer_first: A gets to the plan faster: 'I'll lay out a plan with sensible defaults' then immediately maps gaps; B opens with 'recall lookup and the clarifying questions got blocked,' which delays the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to one default stack: 'Recommend a dedicated #alerts Slack channel' and 'Sentry', 'Better Stack / UptimeRobot', 'Healthchecks.io'; B keeps more branches like 'Healthchecks.io or Better Stack' and 'Grafana Cloud free tier (or Better Stack)'.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent context, but B invents more specific details: 'prod 사주/천기문 web app', 'PM2/launchd bridge, docbroker, agent fleet', and 'evidence-verifier agent'. A also assumes 'Next.js-ish web app, Aurora PostgreSQL + Supabase'.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it gives a tighter Phase 0/1 sequence and concrete monitored surfaces: 'key URLs (homepage, login... payment callback)' plus 'One curl at job end'.
